meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Freelancers: Debugging with AI to Solve Stuck Projects
content.seo_description: Break free from basic app limitations. Learn how to master AI-driven debugging using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver professional SaaS faster.
content.answer_block: Stop fighting cryptic stack traces. By integrating Antigravity for architectural analysis and Claude Code for iterative repairs, you transform debugging from a bottleneck into a competitive advantage. This systematic approach allows you to identify root causes in seconds, ensuring your client projects remain profitable, scalable, and error-free.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal that refuses to cooperate, your client deadline is looming, and that simple feature you promised has turned into a labyrinth of dependency hell. We’ve all been there—the 'tutorial purgatory' where your apps work on your machine but crumble the moment you try to scale. It’s the silent killer of freelance momentum, leaving you stuck in a loop of endless bug fixes instead of shipping high-value features.

But the era of manual debugging is over. By leveraging the cognitive power of AI-native tooling, you can offload the mental tax of parsing thousands of lines of code. This isn't just about fixing a bug; it's about shifting your development workflow from 'trial and error' to 'precision engineering,' allowing you to ship professional-grade SaaS applications while your competitors are still reading documentation.

steps.title: Isolate the Architectural Failure
steps.description: Before patching, identify where the logic breaks. Use Antigravity to map your dependency graph and pinpoint circular references or bottlenecks causing the failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase and generate a dependency map. Highlight any modules that have high coupling or circular dependencies contributing to runtime instability.

steps.title: Contextualize the Crash with Claude
steps.description: Feed the stack trace and relevant files into Claude Code to get an immediate root cause analysis rather than guessing at the error.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the attached stack trace. Explain the logical failure in plain English and propose a refactoring plan that avoids this pattern in the future.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Explain why this stack trace occurs in the context of my current authentication flow.'

steps.title: Generate Precise Fixes
steps.description: Use AI to write the patch, ensuring it aligns with your existing code style and doesn't introduce regression errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a refactored version of the problematic function. Ensure it handles edge cases and follows industry-standard error handling practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude repair --file src/auth/login.ts --reason 'Fixing async race condition on session initialization'

steps.title: Automated Regression Testing
steps.description: Verify your fix doesn't break dependent modules by running an automated suite against the new code structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of unit tests for the newly patched module that covers both successful paths and common failure states.

steps.title: Refactor for Production Hardening
steps.description: Once the bug is squashed, use AI to optimize the code for performance and readability, making the project maintainable for future client handoffs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the patched code for performance optimizations. Suggest clean code improvements that reduce technical debt without altering functionality.

pro_tips: Always provide the AI with your package.json or lockfile; it helps Claude understand the environment constraints and version conflicts.
Use Antigravity to visualize 'hot paths' in your code; these are usually where your bugs are hiding.
When using Claude Code, use the --no-interactive flag in CI/CD pipelines to force deterministic debugging steps.
Ask the AI to generate a 'README-update' snippet alongside your fix to keep documentation in sync with your code changes.
Chain your prompts: ask for the analysis first, then the plan, and only then the implementation to minimize hallucinations.
content.next_steps: Integrate Claude Code into your local Git hooks to automatically trigger analysis whenever a test suite fails, effectively automating your QA process.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical edge to stop debugging and start building. Don't let technical debt dictate your freelance career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
